rationale.R1.1: Section contains an explicit contribution claim ('first preference-trained image-level router'), a gap statement (GT supervision avoided via inter-detector disagreement), and a thesis; all are subject to reconstruction and scrutiny under a technical review reading.
rationale.R1.2: The causal chain — unsupervised preference signal → router training → FLOP savings with quality retention — must cohere across the four paragraphs; logical gaps or unsupported inferential steps are central failure modes.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete numeric claims (53.4 mAP at 38% FLOPs, 0.4 mAP gap on LVIS, 184ms→71ms latency) need to be evaluated for alignment with the experimental setup as described; evidence-claim tension is a primary review target.
rationale.R1.4: The final paragraph asserts 'first' priority for the preference-trained detection router; validity of this novelty claim against BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet, and image-level routing prior work is load-bearing for a technical review but irrelevant for a pure writing review.
